title: Machine vision system
abstract: A machine vision system comprising receiving means configured to receive image data indicative of an object to be classified where there is provided processing means with an initial neural network, the processing means configured to determine a differential equation describing the initial neural network algorithm based on the neural network parameters, and to determine a solution to the differential equation in the form of a series expansion; and to convert the series expansion to a finite series expansion by limiting the number of terms in the series expansion to a finite number; and to determine the output classification in dependence on the finite series expansion.
claims:
Claims 1. A machine vision system, comprising: receiving means configured to receive image data indicative of an object to be classified; and, processing means provided with an initial neural network algorithm having an input layer provided with the received image data, at least one hidden layer and an output layer, the at least one hidden layer including a plurality of neurons each having an associated activation function, the processing means being configured to provide an output classification to the output layer In dependence on the received image data, wherein the processing means is configured to: determine a differential equation describing the initial neural network algorithm based on the activation functions; determine a solution to the differential equation in the form of a series expansion; convert the series expansion to a finite series expansion by limiting the number of terms in the series expansion to a finite number; and, determine the output classification in dependence on the finite series expansion. 2. A machine vision system according to Claim 1, wherein the processing means is configured to: inspect each of the activation functions to determine whether they are differentiable; and, replace each of the activation functions that are not differentiable with a differentiable approximation to the activation function, the determined differential equation being based on the differentiable activation functions or their approximations thereof. 3. A machine vision system according to Claim 2, wherein the one or more of the activation functions is a rectifier function, and wherein the processing means is configured to replace the rectifier function with a sigmoid function. 4. A machine vision system according to Claim 2 or Claim 3, wherein the one or more of the activation functions is a max pooling layer function, and wherein the processing means is configured to replace the max pooling layer function with a weighted sum of elements. 5. A machine vision system according to any of Claims 2 to 4, wherein the one or more of the activation functions is a non-linear activation function, and wherein the processing means is configured to replace the non-linear activation function with a Chen-Fliess series equivalent system. 6. A machine vision system according to any previous claim, wherein the processing means is configured to evaluate the received input data using the finite series expansion to provide the output classification. 7. A machine vision system according to any of Claims 1 to 5, wherein the processing means is configured to transform the finite series expansion into an updated neural network algorithm, and to evaluate the received input data using the updated neural network to provide the output classification. 8. A machine vision system according to any previous claim, wherein the processing means Is configured to determine a state space form of the differential equation so as to determine the series expansion. 9. A machine vision system according to Claim 8, wherein the processing means is configured to extract one or more vector fields from the state space form so as to determine the series expansion. 10. A machine vision system according to any previous claim, wherein the processing means is configured to convert the initial neural network algorithm to a differentiable approximation thereof, and to determine the differential equation based on the differentiable approximation. 11. A machine vision system according to Claim 10, wherein for a fully-connected layer of the initial neural network algorithm the differentiable approximation is of the form: where x is a latent state, u is a network input, b is a bias, Ï is a vector of instances of an activation function, h > 0, k is the layer of the initial neural network algorithm, and A and B are state and input transfer matrices. 12. A machine vision system according to Claim 10 or Claim 11, wherein for a convolutional layer of the initial neural network algorithm the differentiable approximation is of the form: where X Is a layer state matrix, U is a layer input matrix, Â£ is a bias, and C and D are state and input convolution filters. 13. A machine vision system according to any of Claims 10 to 12, wherein the processing means is configured to differentiate the differentiable approximation so as to obtain the differential equation. 14. A machine vision system according to Claim 13, wherein the processing means is configured to apply a numerical method to differentiate the differentiable approximation. 15. A machine vision system according to any previous claim, wherein the processing means is configured to determine one or more coefficients of the series expansion using a Chen-Fliess series expansion. 16. A machine vision system according to any previous claim, wherein the processing means Is configured to determine a window of convergence of the series expansion. 17. A machine vision system according to any previous claim, wherein the processing means is configured to calculate a minimal basis to generate the series expansion. 18. A machine vision system according to any previous claim, wherein the processing means is configured to determine a different set of coefficients of the series expansion for each of a plurality of time windows. 19. A machine vision system according to Claim 18, wherein the processing means is configured to adjust the determined coefficients to adjust vector field parameters of the differential equation until a predetermined level of convergence is achieved. 20. A machine vision system according to any previous claim, wherein the processing means is configured to update the finite series expansion in dependence on the received Image data and the associated output classification as part of a re- training process. 21. A machine vision system, comprising: receiving means configured to receive training data including image data indicative of an object and an associated known classification of the object; and, processing means provided with an initial neural network algorithm having an Input layer provided with the received image data, at least one hidden layer and an output layer, the at least one hidden layer including a plurality of neurons each having an associated activation function, each connection between two of the neurons having an associated synaptic weight, and the processing means being configured to determine appropriate values of the synaptic weights to ensure that evaluating the initial neural network algorithm with the received image data produces the associated known classification, wherein the processing means is configured to: determine a differential equation describing the initial neural network algorithm based on the activation functions; determine a solution to the differential equation in the form of a series expansion; convert the series expansion to a finite series expansion by limiting the number of terms in the series expansion to a finite number; determine the output classification in dependence on the finite series expansion; and, adjust the values of the synaptic weights so as to reduce the error between the determined output classification and the associated known classification of the object. 22. A machine vision control system for configuring a manufacturing process component/system, the machine vision control system comprising: 

 receiving means configured to receive image data indicative of an object to be classified; and, processing means provided with an initial neural network algorithm having an input layer provided with the received image data, at least one hidden layer and an output layer, the at least one hidden layer including a plurality of neurons each having an associated activation function, the processing means being configured to provide an output classification to the output layer in dependence on the received image data; and, control means configured to send a control signal to the manufacturing process component/system in dependence on the output classification so as to configure a setup of the manufacturing process component/system, wherein the processing means is configured to: determine a differential equation describing the neural network algorithm based on the activation functions; determine a solution to the differential equation in the form of a series expansion; convert the series expansion to a finite series expansion by limiting the number of terms in the series expansion to a finite number; and, determine the output classification in dependence on the finite series expansion. 23. A production line arrangement for inspecting a production line component to identify and locate damage, foreign objects and/or incorrectly fitted parts of the production line component, the production line arrangement comprising: an optical system including at least one camera configured to capture one or more sets of image data relating to the production line component; a machine vision control system comprising receiving means configured to receive image data indicative of an object to be classified, and processing means provided with an initial neural network algorithm having an input layer provided with the received image data, at least one hidden layer and an output layer, the at least one hidden layer including a plurality of neurons each having an associated 

 activation function, the processing means being configured to provide an output classification to the output layer in dependence on the received image data; and, a manufacturing process component/system configured to adjust a set up thereof in dependence on the output classification, wherein the processing means is configured to: determine a differential equation describing the neural network algorithm based on the activation functions; determine a solution to the differential equation in the form of a series expansion; convert the series expansion to a finite series expansion by limiting the number of terms in the series expansion to a finite number; and, determine the output classification in dependence on the finite series expansion. 24. A machine vision method, comprising: receiving image data indicative of an object to be classified; providing an initial neural network algorithm having an input layer provided with the received image data, at least one hidden layer and an output layer, the at least one hidden layer including a plurality of neurons each having an associated activation function; determining a differential equation describing the initial neural network algorithm based on the activation functions and other associated neural network parameters; determining a solution to the differential equation in the form of a series expansion; converting the series expansion to a finite series expansion by limiting the number of terms in the series expansion to a finite number; and, 

 providing an output classification to the output layer in dependence on the received image data, wherein the output classification is determined in dependence on the finite series expansion. 25. A computer program product storing instructions thereon that when executed by one or more electronic processors causes the one or more processors to perform the method of Claim 24. 26. A tablet device comprising the machine vision system of any of Claims 1 to 21.
